text: Exactly. It's like this with games as well. If you're new to a franchise, lots of "hardcore" gamers will call you noob for making the very same mistakes they themselves made in the start. That's how many possible new gamers get away from video games, and the gaming industry doesn't get new consumers. I wish there would be more considerate people out there, who know how to respect newcomers.

Balsamic Vinegar. 

oh wait, they translated Lucky Star into English?
..Wait, what? o____0 

There's half the fun gone, right there. unlike most people I like most of the english dubs that are out...
but after having listened to it in japanese first... I can't imagine it any other way. 
most english voice actors are too...
 fake-sounding, y'know? or too prolific. 

e.g They should have just kicked Firion out of Dissidia and put Ichigo Kurosaki in. 
game would be ten times more awesome. >_______>

text:
Jesus, reinserting my brain after that.

She's cute in a childish sense, almost to the point of being mentally underdeveloped. I really hope these impossibly shy and pure types aren't REALLY as common in Japan as anime's seem to suggest.

And I never get the whole thing with Balsamic vinegar having a "stink". That's great stuff, perfect with oil and bread. Amazing that the native eaters of sushi and other bizarre dishes think a common VINEGAR has a notable odor...
